title: 1.1: States that the queue stores pending messages in a configured location directory (e.g., /tmp/maddy-queue-test/queue/ or the path specified in the queue location directive)
1.2: Identifies the file naming pattern as <msg_id>.{meta,header,body} where each message has three separate files (e.g., 37b508e1.meta, 37b508e1.header, 37b508e1.body).

1.3: States that retry timing of message is calculated from when the delivery attempt actually starts, not from when it was queued (e.g., next_try_delay computed after semaphore acquisition, not from queue submission time)
1.4: States that the .meta file contains TriesCount field showing the number of delivery attempts (e.g., \"TriesCount\": 1 after first failed attempt)

1.5: Identifies that the .meta file contains FirstAttempt timestamp, LastAttempt timestamp showing when each delivery was attempted (e.g., 'FirstAttempt': '2026-01-09T10:13:07.131647167Z', 'LastAttempt': '2026-01-09T10:13:07.185435604Z')

1.6: Reports that the .meta file contains RcptErrs field mapping recipients to SMTP error details including Code, EnhancedCode, Message (e.g., \"Code\": 451, \"EnhancedCode\": [4, 0, 0], \"Message\": \"Temporary failure, please retry later\")

1.7: States that log entries for retry attempts include fields: msg_id, smtp_code, smtp_enchcode, smtp_msg, attempts_count, next_try_delay (e.g., queue: will retry {\"attempts_count\":1,\"msg_id\":\"37b508e1\",\"next_try_delay\":\"14m59.999999338s\"})

1.8: Identifies the delivery attempt sequence as: delivery attempt #N logged, failure logged if unsuccessful, \"will retry\" message scheduled for temporary errors, subsequent attempts numbered incrementally (e.g., \"delivery attempt #1\" followed by \"will retry\" then \"delivery attempt #2\")
1.9: Reports that when a server accepts connections but never responds, maddy waits indefinitely because no read timeout is configured (e.g., delivery attempt still hanging after 3+ minutes)
1.10: States that maddy attempts deliveries to different destinations in parallel using a semaphore mechanism (e.g., \"queue: delivery semaphore acquired for bf4f6ced\" in logs)

1.11: Reports that queue starvation can occur when all max_parallelism slots are blocked on hanging servers, causing queued messages to wait for semaphore acquisition (e.g., observable when parallelism slots are consumed by non-responsive destinations)
2.1: Claims that failed messages are stored in a dead-letter directory or bounce folder after max_tries exhaustion (e.g., stating messages go to /queue/dead-letter/ or /queue/failed/)
